data.title: Microsoft stock is down 17% YTD, worst among major stocks. Underperformed S&P 500 by 30% since 2025. Shows AI's impact.
data.description: Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) is currently the worst-performing stock among the "Magnificent Seven" in 2026, with a 17% year-to-date decline. Its underperformance against the broader market has been over 30% since early August 2025, marking the worst relative performance since the dot-com bubble in 2000. Microsoft has underperformed the S&P 500 for seven consecutive months, with a 6% negative relative gap in February 2026. This losing streak is unprecedented in Microsoft's 40-year public market history. The company's relative strength compared to the market is at its lowest level since November 2022, when OpenAI launched ChatGPT, sparking the AI boom. This downturn raises questions about whether the market is pricing AI out of Microsoft's story. Veteran Wall Street strategist Ed Yardeni attributes this sell-off to Microsoft's aggressive capital expenditures.

data.title: Sagtec expands AI SaaS strategy as enterprise pipeline grows; aims for AI-native recurring revenue.
data.description: Sagtec Global Limited, a provider of AI-native enterprise SaaS and automation platform solutions, revealed on February 19, 2026, that its revenue-generating AI ecosystem is expanding due to increased adoption by enterprises in various industry sectors. The company, listed on NASDAQ as SAGT, is experiencing accelerated growth as more businesses utilize its services.

data.title: QuidelOrtho Corp launched episode 56 on Science Bytes podcast featuring Dr. Lindy Carlstrom, discussing high-sensitivity troponin for faster cardiac answers.
data.description: San Diego-based QuidelOrtho Corporation, a prominent player in the field of in vitro diagnostics and listed on Nasdaq under the symbol QDEL, recently unveiled the latest edition of its Science Bytes podcast. The episode, number 56, showcases Dr. Lindy Carlstrom, who serves as the Global Medical Affairs Manager for Acute Care within the company. In this engaging podcast, Dr. Carlstrom dives into discussions with the host on various pertinent topics...

data.title: Henderson Park and Green Room Partners team up to buy 288-unit Pointe Grand community in Charleston.
data.description: In Charleston, South Carolina on February 19, 2026, Henderson Park, an international private equity real estate firm, announced their US headquarters in the city. They partnered with Green Room Partners (GRP), a local real estate development and investment firm from Charleston. Henderson Park is known for its global presence, while GRP specializes in acquisitions and developments. This collaboration signifies a significant move in the real estate sector, enhancing opportunities for growth and investment. The two firms are set to combine their expertise and resources to pursue lucrative projects, marking a milestone in both companies' histories.

data.title: 75% of women are skipping healthcare due to time constraints and outdated systems - Teal Health's Report State of Her Health 2026.
data.description: Teal Health recently released a report titled "State of Her Health 2026," indicating that factors such as time constraints, a lack of access, and reliance on outdated systems are causing women to forego seeking medical care. The report highlights that these issues are particularly prevalent in the current healthcare system and are pushing women away from receiving necessary health services. Based on the data, it is evident that a significant number of women are facing challenges in obtaining adequate and timely healthcare. Key findings from the report underscore the urgency for improvements in the healthcare sector to cater to the specific needs of women.

data.title: Prof. John A. Kellum joins FIZE Medical's Advisory Board focusing on smart fluid management in critical care.
data.description: A well-known expert in critical care has been appointed to aid in the development of early prediction and intervention strategies for fluid management in the intensive care unit (ICU). Recently, FIZE Medical, a company specializing in smart fluid management technology for critical care, disclosed the hiring of Prof. John. This move signals the company's commitment to enhancing ICU practices. The announcement was made on February 19, 2026, in New York.

data.title: MediStreams had a successful 2025, expanding in healthcare payment automation. Major achievements in end-to-end remittance processing.
data.description: In Atlanta, MediStreams, a prominent healthcare payment and remittance automation solutions provider, had a successful year in 2025. They achieved record-breaking numbers while enhancing the industry's sole comprehensive remittance processing solution. Their offerings include medical lockbox services, EOB and correspondence conversion, and automated reconciliation throughout the revenue cycle. The company's genuine end-to-end reconciliation platform has garnered appreciation from clients and partners for its effectiveness in combining EOB conversion and other key services.

data.title: Badge secures $17.1 million funding to empower Apple and Google Wallets as key customer interfaces.
data.description: Badge, a company based in San Francisco, has recently secured $17.1 million in funding. The company's goal is to elevate Apple and Google Wallets as the upcoming prominent customer interface.

data.title: Dairy Queen partners with Savannah Bananas for 2026 season, offering flips, fun, & free shakes. Unforgettable fan experiences.
data.description: In Minneapolis, American Dairy Queen Corporation (ADQ) is set to kick off the 2026 Banana Ball season with excitement. The notable brand known for its iconic treats and food has partnered with the Savannah Bananas to offer fans an unforgettable experience. The collaboration will feature national TV commercials and engaging social media content throughout the season, enhancing the fun with flips and delicious treats. This partnership aims to create lasting memories for fans of both brands, marking an exciting new chapter in their shared journey.

data.title: 8x8 launches AI-powered solution Smart Assist for contact centers, aims to improve resolution, CX, and agent satisfaction.
data.description: Today, in Campbell, California, 8x8, Inc. announced the launch of 8x8 Smart Assist. This new AI-powered solution, from the company listed on NASDAQ as EGHT, offers real-time guidance, dynamic workflows, and intelligent automation.

data.title: Doc.com expands telemedicine to Florida, advancing AI and blockchain healthcare access. Milestone in US growth, residents can connect with licensed professionals.
data.description: In a recent announcement, Doc.com, a leading healthcare tech company, revealed the introduction of its telemedicine platform in Florida. This launch is a key step in its continuous expansion across the United States. The platform enables Florida residents to access licensed healthcare professionals through a user-friendly mobile interface. This move signifies a crucial advancement in the company's nationwide expansion strategy.

data.title: CD Valet survey shows 33% of banks offer digital CD account opening.
data.description: A new digital marketplace called CD Valet, based in Seattle, has been launched to link consumers with top verified high-yield CD rates across the country. This initiative aims to support community financial institutions in drawing new deposits successfully. The company shared findings from its recent survey conducted on Feb. 19, 2026, demonstrating its impact.

data.title: Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition for Nintendo Switch 2 brings enhanced visuals, available today on Nintendo eShop.
data.description: In Redmond, Washington, the Xenoblade Chronicles X: Definitive Edition - Nintendo Switch 2 Edition game has been released for the Nintendo Switch 2 system. It is now available on Nintendo eShop and the My Nintendo Store digitally. Gamers can enjoy a vast, planet-sized journey filled with exciting battles and massive mech-like Skells. The Nintendo Switch 2 Edition offers enhancements such as reaching up to a 4K resolution at a consistent frame rate of up to 60 fps when played in TV mode, and up to 1080p resolution and 60 fps in handheld mode.
